description: 19 - 16-01-2025 - Emails between Gerard McGurk (Head of Security & Resilience  Department, FCDO) and others No action required- just sharing so that you know what I have said.  ---------- Forwarded me
text:
Department, FCDO) and others
No action required- just sharing so that you know what I have said. 
---------- Forwarded message --------- 
From: 
 <
> 
Date: Thu, 16 Jan 2025 at 14:26 
Subject: Re: Urgent vetting question 
To: Gerard McGurk <
> 
Cc: 
 <
>, Ian Collard * 
 <
>, Tom 
Bramley <
>, Patricia Dreghorn 
<
>, Michael Roberts 
<
> 
Hello 
I have just had a call with the VCO and our case team. The case is progressing and both a 
referee and line manager have now been interviewed. Whilst the interview with Lord M was 
scheduled for tomorrow we still await a number of outstanding documents and information. 
The VCO is in contact with 
 - FCDO Head of USA Global Issues and they have 
agreed that it is possible to reschedule the interview for next Friday, at which point all 
missing information should be provided. We are told that this fits with the other diary 
commitments and is acceptable and so we are awaiting confirmation on this from 
 
 later this afternoon. 
We are not seeking to delay unnecessarily but there is a need to ensure that the interview 
adds value and currently I understand we do not have sufficient information in some areas to 
be able to do that. 
I have asked for a further update as soon as we hear back from 
. 
thanks 
On Thu, 16 Jan 2025 at 13:49, Gerard McGurk <
> wrote: 
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE - PERSONAL DATA 
Thanks 
. The primary issue appears to be one of time or the lack of it 
relative to Lord Mandelson trying to get 101 things done in a very short period of 
time (including an audience with the King) before he departs for Washington. I think 
he essentially has a two week window to get everything done before flying to the 
US. We have made the point to him about being upfront and detailed about his 
contacts. 
Personal
JCS
JCS

JCS
JCS

Personal
Personal

55

Gerard

OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE - PERSONAL DATA.

From JCS < PERSONAL S

Sent: 16 January 2025 13:32

to: JCS < PERSONAL >

Cc: Gerard McGurk < PERSONAL >; lan Collard*< = PERSONAL
Personal Tom Bramley

< PERSONAL >; Patricia Dreghorn < PERSONAL

Subject: Re: Urgent vetting question

Good afternoon

>,Jes

Iam urgently seeking an update on this from the VCO involved and I will come back as

soon as possible to clarify the situation.

thanks
JCS
On Thu, 16 Jan 2025 at 13:26, JCS Es PERSONAL > wrote:
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE
Gerard

This is the reply when | chased yesterday on whether an interview had been
arranged:

56


Please see below response from the Special Handling Team. 
We are still waiting for documents to be completed that are required prior to the 
interview.  As far as I know, the VCO is looking to get him booked in next week hopefully 
for the interview, but this is dependent on us receiving all that we need. 
 
 | Head of Personnel Security Team 
Cluster 4 Security, Estates Security and Resilence Department, ESND 
  | Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office, King 
Charles St  | London   | SW1A 2AH 
Telephone:  Mobile: 
 | email:       
A-Z Guidance - Developed Vetting (DV) and Security Check (SC) clearances
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE 
From: Gerard McGurk 
  
Sent: 16 January 2025 13:07 
To: Ian Collard * 
; 
 
; 
 <
> 
Cc: Tom Bramley 
; Patricia Dreghorn 
<

Subject: RE: Urgent vetting question  
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE 
Dear 
 and colleagues, 
JCS
JCS
JCS
JCS
JCS
PERSONAL
PERSONAL
PERSONAL
PERSONAL
PERSONAL
PERSONAL
PERSONAL
PERSONAL
Personal
57


Our FCDO Americas Director has told us there is a possibility that Lord
Mandelson's DV interview tomorrow may be cancelled because UKSV require
more information on who he knows. Lord Mandelson will be looking to try to
expand on this requirement later this afternoon.

It remains important the interview happens tomorrow. There's not another slot and
UKSV colleagues talking through with him during the interview will help get
answers, with the possibility of follow up at a later stage.

Could you confirm the DV interview will still proceed?

Thanks.

Gerard

Gerard McGurk (he/him) MBE|Deputy Director and Senior Security Adviser

Head of Security & Resilience Department | Estates, Security & Network
Directorate | Personal | PERSONAL for diary requests |

parsing_notes: Extract structured email headers (From, To, Cc, Subject, Date/Sent) for each message in the chain. Identify reply/forward nesting depth. Parse each message body separately. Redacted addresses appear as 'PERSONAL' — names are retained. Build sender→recipient edges per message for communication graph.
